app.name: LiteFoil
shutdown.title: Exiting...
catalog.statusDeferred: Library not available in this mode.
catalog.statusAppletBlocked: Remote library loading is disabled on applet mode.
catalog.statusNotConfigured: Configure catalogUrl in sdmc:/switch/LiteFoil/config.json or a local config.json to load a remote index.
catalog.statusParseFailed: The remote index was downloaded, but the JSON could not be parsed.
dialog.appletModeBody: LiteFoil is running in applet mode. Downloads and installations are disabled in this mode.
dialog.appletModeBlockedBody: Downloads and installations are unavailable while LiteFoil is running in applet mode.
catalog.remoteCountSuffix: remote entries from the current source.
catalog.emptyRemoteSubtitle: The library loaded, but no storefront-ready title metadata was found yet.
catalog.emptyUpdateSubtitle: No remote updates were matched against the installed titles yet.
section.installed.title: Installed Games
section.installed.subtitle: List of installed games.
section.recommended.title: Recommended
section.recommended.subtitle: Games recommended by critic data.
section.newGames.title: New Games
section.newGames.subtitle: Browse the remote library entries.
section.updates.title: Updates
section.updates.subtitle: List of available updates to your installed games.
section.dlcs.title: DLCs
section.dlcs.subtitle: List of available DLCs to your installed games.
section.queue.title: Queue List
section.queue.subtitle: Manage the downloads queue list.
section.settings.title: Settings
section.settings.subtitle: App general settings.
hero.installed.title: Scan the console first.
hero.installed.subtitle: LiteFoil should know what is already on the device as soon as it boots.
hero.installed.body: Installed Games now shows only real console entries, with no placeholder cards.
hero.installed.loadedSubtitle: These entries were read directly from the console at startup.
hero.catalogNotConfigured.title: Configure a remote library first.
hero.catalogNotConfigured.subtitle: LiteFoil is ready to fetch a Tinfoil custom_index once catalogUrl is set.
hero.catalogNotConfigured.body: Add a source URL to sdmc:/switch/LiteFoil/config.json or a local config.json and reboot the app to populate New Games and Updates.
hero.newGames.title: New releases in a clean storefront shell.
hero.newGames.subtitle: The first online catalog pass will read the detailed Tinfoil custom index JSON format.
hero.newGames.body: Later phases will resolve cover art, package formats and source-specific actions without disturbing the shell.
hero.newGames.loadedSubtitle: The storefront is now fed by the configured custom_index library.
hero.newGames.emptySubtitle: The transport is working, but the index still needs richer title data for storefront cards.
hero.updates.title: Spot outdated installs quickly.
hero.updates.subtitle: The update view is designed for side-by-side version comparison and queueing.
hero.updates.body: Once platform services are wired in, this page will show current version, target version and queue status per title.
hero.updates.loadedTitle: Updates detected from the remote source.
hero.updates.loadedSubtitle: Installed versions are now being compared against the downloaded library.
hero.updates.emptySubtitle: Either the library is current or the index does not expose matching title metadata yet.
hero.updates.emptyBody: Try another catalog source or verify that the remote metadata uses the same title IDs as the installed library.
hero.dlcs.title: Group DLCs around their base games.
hero.dlcs.subtitle: The layout already reserves space for badges, bundle names and content type labels.
hero.dlcs.body: The next iterations can distinguish owned content, missing add-ons and optional bundles from the same feed.
hero.dlcs.loadedTitle: DLCs detected from the remote source.
hero.dlcs.loadedSubtitle: The storefront is now surfacing add-ons from the current library feed.
hero.dlcs.emptySubtitle: Either the source is not exposing add-ons or the visible entries are already installed.
hero.dlcs.emptyBody: Try another source or refresh the catalog to expand the DLC listing.
hero.queue.title: A single queue for online installs.
hero.queue.subtitle: Downloads, decompression and install steps will share one predictable workflow.
hero.queue.body: Phase 0 only previews the surface, but the final queue will persist between launches and survive recoverable failures.
hero.queue.loadedSubtitle: The persistent queue already keeps what should be downloaded and installed next.
hero.settings.title: Tune language, theme and library behavior.
hero.settings.subtitle: Settings centralize display, source management and local database maintenance.
hero.settings.body: This section now previews the controls planned for language, theme, connections and offline metadata refresh.
installed.emptySlotSubtitle: Additional entries will appear here as the library grows.
cards.newGames.1.title: Remote catalog card
cards.newGames.1.subtitle: Designed for cover art, package type and title metadata.
cards.newGames.2.title: Google Drive source
cards.newGames.2.subtitle: Reserved for provider-specific handling and friendly source labels.
cards.newGames.3.title: Direct HTTP source
cards.newGames.3.subtitle: This shell already leaves room for source badges and queue actions.
cards.updates.1.title: Version comparison card
cards.updates.1.subtitle: Shows installed version beside the newest catalog version.
cards.updates.2.title: Queue-aware state
cards.updates.2.subtitle: The same title can move from available to queued without leaving the page.
cards.updates.3.title: Readable release notes
cards.updates.3.subtitle: Reserved space for build number, package size and changelog snippets.
cards.dlcs.1.title: Expansion grouping
cards.dlcs.1.subtitle: Bundle DLC cards underneath a game-focused heading.
cards.dlcs.2.title: Content label support
cards.dlcs.2.subtitle: Extra packs, season passes and upgrades can share the same layout.
cards.dlcs.3.title: Queue directly from detail cards
cards.dlcs.3.subtitle: Prepared for future install CTA once catalog actions are connected.
cards.queue.1.title: Active transfer
cards.queue.1.subtitle: Current item, speed and ETA will live in this slot.
cards.queue.2.title: Queued package
cards.queue.2.subtitle: The queue view reserves room for source URL and retry state.
cards.queue.3.title: Resume after reboot
cards.queue.3.subtitle: Future queue storage can repopulate the screen after relaunch.
cards.settings.1.title: Language
cards.settings.1.subtitle: Choose the desired interface language.
cards.settings.2.title: Theme
cards.settings.2.subtitle: Choose the desired visual theme.
cards.settings.installationModel.title: Installation model
cards.settings.installationModel.subtitl: Choose whether installs use a local download or direct streaming.
cards.settings.4.title: Connections
cards.settings.4.subtitle: Manage your remote library connection.
cards.settings.5.title: Unsigned sources
cards.settings.5.subtitle: Manage the policy to install unsigned sources.
dialog.installedPreview: Installed Games is still using placeholder cards, but the shell is now ready to receive title parsing and local metadata loading.
dialog.recommendedPreview: Recommended appears when the remote index exposes critic-backed recommendations matching downloadable files.
dialog.newGamesPreview: New Games now fetches the configured custom_index source on demand. The next step is richer storefront presentation, covers and actions.
dialog.updatesPreview: Available Updates already compares installed versions against the fetched catalog when title metadata matches. The next step is queue actions and richer details.
dialog.dlcsPreview: Available DLCs already has its own shell and will later group add-ons by parent title.
dialog.queuePreview: Install Queue is prepared for a persistent online-only workflow with download, retry and install states.
dialog.settingsPreview: Settings will become the home for language, source URLs, offline metadata refresh and installer defaults.
dialog.exitWhileQueueActiveBody: A download or installation is still running. Exit anyway?
dialog.queueRequiresBaseInstalled: Install the base title first before queueing its update or DLC.
dialog.searchBody: Dedicated library search lands in the next step. The shortcut and quick filters are already reserved in the shell.
dialog.searchGuide: Type to filter the current section. Leave it empty to clear.
dialog.searchUnavailable: The software keyboard could not be opened on this device.
appUpdate.title: LiteFoil Update
appUpdate.busy: Finish downloads or installations before updating the app.
appUpdate.success: Update finished. LiteFoil will close; open the app again to use the new version.
dialog.queueAdded: Item added to the install queue.
dialog.queueBulkConfirmBody: Add all to the install queue?
dialog.queueBulkAdded: Items added to the install queue.
task.queue.title: Downloading queued item
install.error.noConnector: No install connector for this format
install.error.metaDbFailed: Failed to open content meta database
cards.settings.forwarder.title: Install app on home screen
cards.settings.forwarder.subtitle: Install LiteFoil as a shortcut on the Switch home screen.
cards.settings.forwarder.activatedSubtit: LiteFoil is installed on the home screen.
forwarder.dialog.title: Install app on home screen
forwarder.dialog.confirmBody:
This option installs a forwarder on your home screen.
To uninstall it, you must remove it manually.
Do you want to continue?
forwarder.dialog.busyBody: Cannot install while a download or installation is in progress.
forwarder.dialog.successBody:
LiteFoil was installed on the home screen successfully.

Restart the home menu to see the shortcut.
forwarder.dialog.errorBody: Failed to install the forwarder NSP:
forwarder.progress.title: Installing app on home screen
